# 3CH0F0RG3

AI-driven Flipper Zero control from Windows desktop, powered by a Claude Max/Pro subscription. Python port of the Android/Kotlin [V3SP3R](https://github.com/elder-plinius/V3SP3R) (GPL-3.0).

## AI Team Configuration (autogenerated by team-configurator, 2026-04-22)

**Important: YOU MUST USE subagents when available for the task.**

### Detected Stack
- **Language / runtime**: Python `>=3.10`, packaged via `setuptools` + `pyproject.toml` (`src/` layout, package `echoforge`).
- **Async**: `anyio` (plus `pytest-asyncio` for tests).
- **Hardware I/O**: `pyserial` USB-CDC serial link to Flipper Zero; custom RPC frame codec over Flipper protobuf (`flipper.proto`, `application.proto`, `gpio.proto`, `gui.proto`, `storage.proto`, `system.proto`, `desktop.proto`, `property.proto`).
- **Protobuf tooling**: `protobuf>=5.27`, `grpcio-tools` (dev) — generated `*_pb2.py` / `*_pb2.pyi` live in `src/echoforge/proto/`; regenerated via `scripts/fetch_protos.py` + `scripts/build_protos.py`.
- **Agent layer**: `claude-agent-sdk` — MCP tool server, `can_use_tool` hook, streaming `ClaudeSDKClient`, approval handler (`src/echoforge/agent/`).
- **Safety layer**: in-house `risk` / `permissions` / `diff` / `policy` / `audit` modules (`src/echoforge/safety/`).
- **UI**: `textual` + `rich` TUI; no web framework, no frontend, no database.
- **Data models**: `pydantic>=2.8`.
- **Tests**: `pytest` + `pytest-asyncio` under `tests/` (policy, permissions, risk, diff, audit).
- **Lint / format**: `ruff` (`line-length=100`, `target-version=py310`).
- **Packaging**: `pyinstaller` for single-exe Windows build.
- **Entry points**: `echoforge` (CLI, `echoforge.cli:main`) and `echoforge-ping` (`echoforge.tools.ping:main`).
- **Platform**: Windows 10/11 (target); no server, no HTTP API.
- **License**: GPL-3.0-or-later.

### Subagent Routing Table

| Task / Trigger | Subagent | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| New feature design spanning transport + actions + agent + safety | `tech-lead-orchestrator` | Use first for any multi-layer change; it will fan out to specialists below. |
| Idiomatic Python, typing, dataclasses, `anyio`, package layout, `pyproject.toml` | `python-expert` | Primary specialist — most work here lands with this agent. |
| `pytest` + `pytest-asyncio` suites, fixtures, coverage for `safety/`, `transport/`, `agent/` | `Python Testing Expert` (`testing-expert`) | All new modules must ship with tests. |
| Hardening the safety/policy/permissions/audit layer, reviewing `can_use_tool` hooks, handling untrusted Flipper input, serial-level attack surface, Claude-SDK approval flow | `Python Security Expert` (`security-expert`) | Security-critical — a misfire drives real hardware. |
| Serial throughput, RPC framing hot path, protobuf decode cost, TUI refresh rate, async contention | `Python Performance Expert` (`performance-expert`) + `performance-optimizer` | Pair them for profiling + fix. |
| CI, Windows build matrix, PyInstaller packaging, signed-exe release, proto regeneration pipeline | `Python DevOps/CI-CD Expert` (`devops-cicd-expert`) | Handles GitHub Actions and release artifacts. |
| Code review on any PR before merge | `code-reviewer` | Mandatory final pass. |
| Onboarding a new contributor / mapping unfamiliar module | `code-archaeologist` | Use when exploring legacy V3SP3R parity. |
| Updating README, module docstrings, protocol/RPC docs, user-facing CLI help | `documentation-specialist` | |
| Repo-wide "what is this and how does it fit together" audit | `project-analyst` | |
| Any public programmatic interface (MCP tool schema, CLI surface, future IPC) | `api-architect` | Treat MCP tools exposed to Claude as an API. |

### Agents Explicitly Not Applicable
Django / FastAPI / Rails / Laravel / React / Vue / Tailwind / ORM / web-scraping / ML-data experts, and `backend-developer` / `frontend-developer` — this project has **no web layer, no DB, no browser UI, no ML**. Do not route to them; prefer `python-expert` instead.

### Project-Specific Conventions
- **`src/` layout** — never import from outside `src/echoforge/**`; tests import as `from echoforge.safety.policy import ...`.
- **Generated files are committed**: `src/echoforge/proto/*_pb2.py(i)`. Do not hand-edit. Regenerate via `python scripts/build_protos.py` after `scripts/fetch_protos.py`.
- **Safety is non-negotiable**: any new Flipper-mutating action MUST flow through `SafetyPolicy.assess(...)` in `src/echoforge/safety/policy.py` and be logged via `AuditService`. Every write path needs a `DiffSummary`.
- **Claude approval contract**: tools exposed to the agent must declare a risk level; `auto_approve_level` gates auto-allow. Hold-to-confirm (`requires_hold`) is reserved for destructive/irreversible operations.
- **Async everywhere**: use `anyio`, not `asyncio` directly. Transport layer (`serial_link`, `rpc_client`) is async-first.
- **Typing**: Python 3.10+ union syntax (`X | None`), `from __future__ import annotations` in new modules, Pydantic v2 models.
- **Lint gate**: `ruff check` must pass with the repo config (`line-length=100`, `py310` target). No separate formatter — ruff formats.
- **Windows-first**: paths, COM-port enumeration, and packaging assume Windows. Do not introduce POSIX-only deps.
- **License hygiene**: GPL-3.0-or-later inherited from upstream V3SP3R. Any new runtime dep must be GPL-compatible.

### Sample Invocation
> Try: `@python-expert add a reboot-to-DFU action wired through SafetyPolicy with a hold-to-confirm approval`, then `@testing-expert cover it in tests/`, then `@code-reviewer`.

---

## Repository Layout (quick reference)
```
echoforge/
  pyproject.toml            # deps + entry points + ruff config
  protos/                   # upstream Flipper .proto sources
  scripts/                  # fetch_protos.py, build_protos.py
  src/echoforge/
    cli.py (entry)          # `echoforge` command
    flipper.py              # high-level facade
    transport/              # serial_link, rpc_codec, rpc_client, cli_session, client_proxy
    proto/                  # generated *_pb2 / *_pb2.pyi (do not hand-edit)
    actions/                # application, gpio, hardware, storage, system
    safety/                 # risk, permissions, diff, policy, audit
    agent/                  # runner, tools (MCP), prompts, hooks, approval
    tools/                  # ping, repl, explore, ask_claude, hardware_test, cli_test, writetest
  tests/                    # pytest suite (safety layer covered today)
```

## Setup (Windows)
```bash
python -m venv .venv
.venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -e ".[dev]"
python scripts/fetch_protos.py
python scripts/build_protos.py
pytest
python -m echoforge.tools.ping
```
